title: France's powerful attack secures World Cup semi-final spot
subhead: Kylian Mbappé and his teammates dominated the Atlas Lions (2-0), confirming their offensive potential. They will play the winner of Spain vs. Belgium on July 14 in Dallas.

title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives at British Museum for historic loan
French High Council on Climate warns of urgent need to 'scale up' climate policies
'In trying to avoid accusations of exaggerating, the IPCC has instead underestimated the severity of our summers'
subhead: Long accused of alarmism by the far right, the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) has, in fact, been too cautious, underestimating the speed of change and its impacts, says historian Jean-Baptiste Fressoz.

title: Strait of Hormuz deadlock could lead Trump into sporadic war with Iran
subhead: The renewed US and Iranian strikes on Wednesday, despite the memorandum of understanding reached between the two countries, jeopardize hopes for a swift end to the crisis unpopular with the American public.

title: Kanye West, banned from most European countries, receives €4 million grant to perform in Albania
subhead: Barred from entering or performing due to antisemitic and neo-Nazi statements, the American rapper is set to take the stage on July 11 in Tirana. Many protesters are accusing Prime Minister Edi Rama of using the event to serve his own interests.

title: British Museum director on Bayeux Tapestry loan: 'A gesture of confidence, friendship and, above all, trust'
subhead: France has just delivered the Bayeux Tapestry to the United Kingdom, where it will remain on display until 2027. The unprecedented gesture crowns 1,000 years of shared history, writes Nicholas Cullinan, director of the British Museum.

title: France's trees are dying at an increasing rate due to disrupted seasonal patterns

How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

title: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy
subhead: On Marine Le Pen's first campaign trip after being cleared to run for president despite her conviction, her would-be stand-in Jordan Bardella looked stern at her side.

title: Renewed US-Iran hostilities highlight Tehran's deep mistrust of negotiations
subhead: The exchanges of strikes in recent hours have bolstered the arguments of the ultraconservative wing of Iran's theocratic regime, which regards talks with Washington as criminal. The cease-fire is hanging by a thread.

title: 'Imperial obsession and reckless escalation: Vladimir Putin has never appeared so trapped by his fantasies'
Populists' temptation of impunity
'The UAE does not want its strategic partnership with Israel to depend on Benjamin Netanyahu'
